Experience.title: SOC Analyst Intern
description: SOC Track Trainee in an intensive cyber security program funded by the United States Agency for International Development (USAID) in partnership with Green Circle.
Successfully completed 120 training hours and practical cyber security activities inside the Security Operations Center (SOC) track.
Gained hands-on experience in threat monitoring, log analysis, incident response, and understanding SIEM workflows to detect and mitigate security breaches.

Experience.title: Representative
description: Researched and presented breaking technology news, focusing on AI, metaverse, and digital marketing trends to educate diverse audiences.
Increased community tech literacy by translating complex concepts into accessible content, fostering informed participation in digital spaces.

Experience.title: Founder
description: Founded DSC YU to empower students in cybersecurity through firsthand training and mentorship.
Organized workshops on ethical hacking, penetration testing, and digital forensics.
Led efforts to foster digital safety and a secure university environment.

Experience.title: Virtual Reality Specialist
description: As a VR Specialist, I introduced visitors especially students and families to the world of Virtual Reality. I helped set up and operate VR equipment, guided people through exciting simulations, and made sure everyone had a safe and fun experience. I also explained how VR is used in fields like gaming, education, science, and more. My goal was to make technology easy to understand and enjoyable for all ages.

Experience.title: Technical Support
Experience.company: Computer and Information Center At Yarmouk University
description: Provided front-line technical support to university students, diagnosing and resolving operating system, hardware, and software issues efficiently.
​Assisted in administering and troubleshooting Windows Server environments, ensuring system availability and proper user access controls.
Maintained and hardened technical support devices and lab workstations to ensure a secure and stable operating environment across the computer center.
Diagnosed critical system vulnerabilities and applied necessary patches, bridging hands-on IT support with foundational security practices.

Experience.title: Technical Director
description: Directed GDSC YU in empowering students with advanced tech skills through workshops, projects, and events.
Organized coding bootcamps, hackathons, and tech talks in AI, cyber security, cloud computing, and more.
Bridged academic learning with industry demands, fostering innovation and community impact.

Experience.title: Cybersecurity Intern
description: Selected for the 120-hour Upskilling for Cybersecurity training scholarship after passing competitive technical screening exams and interviews.
Committed to a rigorous, full-time daily training schedule (8:00 AM – 4:00 PM) designed to bridge academic theories with real-world industry demands.
• Networking: Mastering network architecture, protocols, and secure data transmission.
• Linux OS: Deep proficiency in system administration, CLI, and environment configuration.
• Cybersecurity Foundations: Defense-in-depth principles, security architecture, and risk management.
• Ethical Hacking: Vulnerability assessment, penetration testing methodologies, and mitigation techniques.
• Digital Forensic Investigation: Analyzing security incidents, log analysis, and tracing digital evidence.
Successfully cleared all final practical assessments and technical examinations required for graduation.
